merger_name: Restaurant Brands and Yum! Brands – Taco Bell restaurants

other_parties.name: Restaurant Brands Australia Holdings Pty Ltd

merger_description:
Restaurant Brands New Zealand Limited (**Restaurant Brands**) and Yum! Brands, Inc. (**Yum**) propose to acquire, via a joint venture (Taco Ventures Pty Ltd (**JV Co**)):

* 9 Taco Bell restaurants from Restaurant Brands’ subsidiary QSR Pty Ltd, and
* 20 Taco Bell restaurants from Collins Foods Subsidiary Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of Collins Foods Limited (**Collins**).

Restaurant Brands will control JV Co and hold a 60% interest, while Gardiner Lane Capital, LLC, a wholly owned subsidiary of Yum, will acquire a 40% interest.

Yum is a NYSE-listed, US-based restaurant company that owns, operates and franchises various fast-food brands and associated restaurants, including KFC, Pizza Hut and Taco Bell. Yum holds equity in and operates around 70 KFC restaurants in Australia but does not operate or hold equity in any Taco Bell or Pizza Hut restaurants in Australia.

Restaurant Brands is a Yum corporate franchisee. In Australia, it operates 9 Taco Bell restaurants, all of which are in NSW, and 74 KFC restaurants.

Collins is an ASX-listed Australian fast-food corporation that owns and operates 294 KFC restaurants and 20 Taco Bell restaurants in Australia, under franchise arrangements with Yum.

events.title: Restaurant Brands and Yum - Taco Bell Restaurants - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Restaurant Brands New Zealand Limited (Restaurant Brands) and
Yum! Brands, Inc.’s (Yum) acquisition, via a joint venture (Taco
Ventures Pty Ltd (JV Co)), of:
▪ 9 Taco Bell restaurants from Restaurant Brands’ subsidiary
QSR Pty Ltd, and
▪ 20 Taco Bell restaurants from Collins Foods Subsidiary Pty
Ltd, a subsidiary of Collins Foods Limited (Collins).
(together, the Acquisition).
Restaurant Brands will control JV Co and hold a 60% interest, while
Gardiner Lane Capital, LLC, a wholly owned subsidiary of Yum, will
acquire a 40% interest.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

Yum is a NYSE-listed, US-based restaurant company that owns,
operates and franchises various fast-food brands and associated
restaurants, including KFC, Pizza Hut and Taco Bell. In Australia,
Yum holds equity in and operates around 70 KFC restaurants but
does not operate or hold equity in any Taco Bell or Pizza Hut
restaurants.
Restaurant Brands is a Yum corporate franchisee that operates 9
Taco Bell restaurants and 74 KFC restaurants in New South Wales.
Collins is an ASX-listed Australian fast-food corporation that owns
and operates 294 KFC restaurants and 20 Taco Bell restaurants in
Australia, under franchise arrangements with Yum.

Yum, Restaurant Brands and Collins are all active in the operation of
quick service restaurants in Australia.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form and information from third partes.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• The market share aggregation arising from the Acquisition is
estimated to be low because JV Co’s ultimate shareholders
(Restaurant Brands and Yum) have similar equity interests in
competing quick service restaurants as the current owners of
the Taco Bell restaurants.
• JV Co would continue to face competition from many alternative
quick service restaurant chains.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.
